title: drymocallis glandulosa subsp. glabrata
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. glabrata, also known as mountain sweetroot or drymocallis, is a perennial herb native to western North America, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are rich in glycosides and have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of mountain sweetroot include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
